commit.message:
Add runtime ZSign integration for JITLess code signing

The loader now automatically signs downloaded payloads using
LiveContainer's bundled ZSign.dylib:

1. Direct dlopen (works if JIT enabled)
2. Ad-hoc sign via ZSigner.adhocSignMachOAtPath + retry
3. Full sign with LC's P12 cert from NSUserDefaults + retry

Each step logs detailed progress. Overlay shows which signing
approach is being attempted. Falls back gracefully if ZSign
isn't available.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve provisioning failures in cloud-init environment

Three bugs caused complete provisioning failure when run via cloud-init:

1. HOME not set: cloud-init runcmd runs without a login shell, so the
   HOME env var is unset. The Coder install script requires HOME to
   resolve cache/install paths.
   Fix: export HOME=\ in bootstrap.sh and setup.sh.

2. Wrong Coder binary path: setup.sh hardcoded ExecStart=/usr/bin/coder
   but Coder installs to /usr/local/bin/coder (--method=standalone).
   Fix: use /usr/local/bin/coder in the systemd unit.

3. Coder running as root: Coder's built-in PostgreSQL refuses to start
   as root. The systemd unit had no User= directive, defaulting to root.
   Fix: create a dedicated 'coder' system user and set User=coder/Group=coder.
   Also switched deprecated CODER_ADDRESS to CODER_HTTP_ADDRESS.

4. Caddy log permission: proxy.sh created /var/log/caddy as root, then
   Caddy (running as caddy user) couldn't write access.log.
   Fix: chown -R caddy:caddy /var/log/caddy after mkdir.

Closes #15

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: sink mute detection, recovery guidance, and auto-unmute safety net (#123)

Four-layer defense against system-level PA sink mute going unnoticed:

1. UI warning: device card shows orange 'Sink muted' status with blocked
   hint and one-click 'Unmute speaker' action when PA sink is muted but
   app-level mute is off

2. Health degradation: compute_device_health_state() reports degraded
   state with sink_muted_at_system_level reason; new SINK_MUTED and
   SINK_UNMUTED device events in status_event_builder

3. Recovery guidance: new sink_system_muted issue in operator recovery
   assistant with 'Unmute speaker' primary action; new /api/unmute_sink
   endpoint for direct PA sink unmute bypassing MA routing

4. Auto-unmute safety net: parent-side watchdog auto-unmutes after 30s
   grace period if desync persists; daemon startup unmute retries up to
   3x on failure with 2s intervals

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use CodeRangeMapRangeList for callcounting stubs and remove unneeded StubManagers (#126521)

In order to figure out if a specific address is a callcounting stub, we
currently have to iterate through the call counting stub allocators to
figure out which one, if any, owns the address. If we switch call
counting stubs to use CodeRangeMapRangeList, the type of the code can
easily be looked up from the cDAC in the RangeSectionMap.

Removed EnumMem of the call counting stub heap. We now have the same
enumeration as other CodeRangeMapRangeList users. That is, the
RangeSection metadata allows lookup of the code type etc, but the actual
bytes of the stubs are not explicitly included in a heap dump.

Relevant for
https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/1d27484034940eee7eb09dec62c5138125346802/src/coreclr/debug/daccess/daccess.cpp#L5441

* fix(ar): improve drag drop position and trash zone deletion

Problem 1: Objects couldn't be dropped everywhere during drag
- Root cause: ARCore hit test only updates position on valid plane surfaces
- Fix: Keep last valid position when hit test fails, object stays in place
- Added lastValidDragPosition to track successful drag positions

Problem 2: Trash zone deletion not working reliably
- Root cause: onDragUpdate only called when currentObj found in placedObjects
- Fix: Always call onDragUpdate even if object not found (use default position)
- Added final trash zone check in onDragEnd to ensure deletion works

Fixes: fix-drag-drop-position, fix-trash-zone-delete
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ar): fix trash zone detection with proper X and Y coordinate check

- Added isOverTrashZone() helper function that checks both X (right side) and Y (bottom) coordinates
- Fixed trash zone hit detection to properly detect when object is dragged to bottom-right corner
- Updated onDragMove and onDragEnd to use the new isOverTrashZone() function
- Constants: TRASH_ZONE_SIZE_DP=80, TRASH_ZONE_MARGIN_DP=16

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: code review fixes and drag improvements

- Fixed magic strings in TrashZone (now uses localized strings)
- Removed unused 'progress' variable
- Added release_to_delete and drag_to_delete strings (EN and TR)
- Improved drag movement with camera ray projection fallback
  - Objects can now be moved to areas without detected planes
  - Uses camera's view matrix to project movement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ar): improve drag drop to work on all screen positions

Root cause: Camera ray projection fallback was using absolute screen
coordinates instead of delta movement, causing erratic object positioning.

Solution:
- Track last screen position (dragLastScreenX/Y) for delta calculation
- Use screen delta movement instead of absolute normalized coordinates
- Project finger movement to world XZ plane using camera vectors
- Apply delta to current position instead of recalculating from scratch
- Scale pixel-to-world conversion based on object distance from camera

Also includes style improvements to TrashZone:
- Add semi-transparent background
- Add subtle shadow effect
- Add subtle border
- Center text properly
- Increase size for better text fit

Fixes: drag-everywhere-bug
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Center text with TextAlign.Center and fillMaxWidth
- Increase size to 100x90dp for better text fit
- Add fillMaxSize to Column for proper centering

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ui): correct trash zone detection bounds for drag-to-delete

Root cause: isOverTrashZone() was using incorrect dimensions (80dp x 80dp)
that didn't match the actual TrashZone component (100dp x 90dp).

Solution: Updated trash zone detection to use the correct dimensions:
- trashZoneWidth = 100.dp (matches component's width)
- trashZoneHeight = 90.dp (matches component's height)
- trashZonePadding = 16.dp

The detection area now correctly matches the visual trash zone position,
enabling reliable drag-to-delete functionality.

Fixes: trash-zone-delete-bug

* feat: add repo-local MCP CLI and planning docs

* fix: normalize SHA/branch tokens in sweep artifact --check to prevent perpetual CI failures

The --check mode regenerates artifacts with the current HEAD SHA and
compares to committed files. Since every push changes the SHA, committed
artifacts are always one commit behind — causing the check to fail
immediately after any commit regardless of content.

Fix: strip volatile tokens (HEAD SHA, branch name in table rows, 'green
on <sha>' CI lines) before diffing. Real content changes (PR number,
descriptions) still cause --check to fail correctly.

backtick-quoted branch names starting with feat/fix/chore/HEAD/main etc.

commit.message:
fix: use absolute positioning for dashboard scroll containers on iOS PWA

Both Dashboard and TaskDashboard had scrollable content (overflow-y-auto)
sitting directly in the flex layout chain. On iOS Safari in standalone
PWA mode, WebKit doesn't properly constrain flex item heights, causing
the content to expand and squish the mobile bottom nav bar to just a
few pixels tall (buttons invisible but still clickable).

The fix uses the same relative/absolute inset-0 PullToRefresh pattern
already working on the Tasks tab, which takes scrollable content out of
flex flow entirely. Also adds pull-to-refresh to the global Dashboard.

* feat: add goal resume command for interrupted goal recovery (AF-STORY-0011)

Add `goal resume` — a command to recover goals silently lost when the
AURA goal loop is interrupted mid-execution (SIGKILL, OOM, Ctrl-C). The
GoalQueue pops goals before execution begins; without this change any
interrupt in the dequeue→archive window permanently discards the goal.

Changes:
- core/in_flight_tracker.py: InFlightTracker writes memory/in_flight_goal.json
  atomically (.tmp + os.replace) immediately after goal dequeue; clear() called
  in finally block after goal_archive.record()
- core/task_handler.py: import InFlightTracker, write tracker before orchestrator,
  wrap execution block in try/finally: tracker.clear()
- aura_cli/options.py: CommandSpec(goal resume), CLIActionSpec(goal_resume),
  _CANONICAL_PATH_TO_ACTION entry
- aura_cli/dispatch.py: _handle_goal_resume_dispatch — reads InFlightTracker,
  prints recovery info, prepend_batch([goal]) to GoalQueue, optionally --run
- aura_cli/cli_options.py: _customize_goal_resume (--run flag), register in
  _PARSER_CUSTOMIZERS
- run_aura.sh: resume) alias → exec_main goal resume

- .aura_forge/backlog/ready/AF-STORY-0011.yaml — full Forge story (RE/RD/SCAMPER/
  AutoTRIZ/SixHats design passes embedded)

commit.message:
fix(storage): harden read_file against unbounded reads (#335)

* fix(storage): bound read_file to prevent OOM on large files

Replace unbounded cat with head -c and add a configurable size limit
(LINUX_MCP_MAX_FILE_READ_BYTES, default 1 MiB) to prevent OOM when
reading multi-GB files or pseudo-files like /proc/kcore.

Defense in depth: pre-check file size via os.path.getsize() (local) or
stat -Lc %s (remote) before reading, plus a sentinel pattern (head
reads limit+1 bytes) as a second barrier.

commit.message:
Fix indirect eval scoping: suppress globalThis writes for direct eval inside functions

When direct eval runs inside a function, var declarations should stay in the
function's variable environment per §19.2.1.3 EvalDeclarationInstantiation.
Previously, DefineGlobal/DefineGlobalSoft/SetGlobal always mirrored non-lexical
bindings to globalThis, causing indirect eval to see the direct eval's local
vars instead of the true global scope.

Added eval_fn_scope flag to VM, set when creating eval VM for direct eval
now check this flag and skip globalThis writes when set.
